title: CIAgent World File
description: A frozen freeze of a failing run's tool traffic (tool arguments to responses) that replays fail-closed. Produced by `ciagent world freeze` and `ciagent world mutate`; consumed by `ciagent simulate --replay --world`. `required` is the intersection of what the loader actually enforces, so both freeze/mutate output and a minimal hand-authored world validate.

world_schema.description: Format version. A loader rejects any value other than the version it implements; a bump is a hard incompatibility, not forward-readable.

frozen_from.description: Provenance of the source envelope (stage id or golden path).

mutated_from.description: Present only on derived worlds (`world mutate`): operator, scoped tools, payload id, source hash.

gaps.description: Tool calls recorded WITHOUT a result at freeze time. They can never match on replay; each surfaces as a fail-closed miss.

tools.description: Per-tool fixtures, keyed by the tool's function name.

sequence.description: When true, matching fixtures are consumed FIFO (state transitions); the same call can return different results over time. When absent/false, fixtures are reusable and no two may share an effective match with differing responses.

suggested_ignore.description: Freeze-time hints (fields that varied between calls, or long free-text) — informational, never auto-applied.

match.description: The frozen call's arguments. A fixture matches when every non-ignored match key is present in the offered args and scalar-equal after the framework's type coercion; extra offered keys are a miss unless they equal the parameter's signature default.

ignore.description: Mutable-field authoring surface: these match keys accept any value (request ids, timestamps, free text).

response.description: The frozen tool result, served verbatim (any JSON type). Absent means null.

turn.description: Informational: which conversation turn this call came from.
